dev_plans.content:
Lightweight heuristic approach: Compute per-subgroup AUROC on the validation set at the end of each epoch and report the absolute gap between best and worst ethnicity group as a single scalar.

What is measured: For each of the 6 ethnicity subgroups (e.g., White, Black, Hispanic, Asian, Native American, Other), compute AUROC using sklearn.metrics.roc_auc_score on the validation predictions filtered to that subgroup. The probe metric is max(AUROC) - min(AUROC) across subgroups.

Pipeline insertion: Add a callback at the end of every epoch in the training loop, after the standard validation pass. The callback receives (y_true, y_pred_proba, ethnicity_labels) for the entire validation set.

Step-by-step:
1. Ensure the validation DataLoader returns ethnicity as part of the batch metadata (extend the Dataset __getitem__ to include the demographic column from MIMIC). Avoid using ethnicity as a model input.
2. After each validation epoch, accumulate y_true, y_pred_proba, and ethnicity into three aligned numpy arrays.
3. For each subgroup g in the 6 ethnicities: filter arrays where ethnicity==g, skip if fewer than 30 positives or 30 negatives (to avoid degenerate AUROC), compute auroc_g = roc_auc_score(y_true_g, y_pred_g).
4. Compute disparity = max(auroc_g) - min(auroc_g) across valid subgroups.
5. Log per-subgroup AUROC and the disparity scalar to TensorBoard / CSV. Also log which group is worst.
6. Plot the disparity as a time series across epochs, with a horizontal threshold line.

Output artifacts: (a) per-epoch CSV row with columns epoch, auroc_white, auroc_black, ..., disparity, worst_group; (b) a matplotlib line chart of disparity vs epoch with threshold; (c) console log line each epoch summarizing the worst group.
dev_plans.metric: Absolute AUROC gap between best- and worst-performing ethnicity subgroup on the validation set per epoch (max_g AUROC_g - min_g AUROC_g).
dev_plans.threshold: 0.10 — A 10 percentage-point AUROC gap is the commonly cited 'large disparity' threshold in the CheXclusion / Roosli literature for clinical models on demographically imbalanced datasets like MIMIC. Tighter thresholds (e.g., 0.05) are aspirational but rarely met by an off-the-shelf cross-entropy MIMIC benchmark model whose smallest ethnicity group has only a few hundred patients, so noise alone produces gaps of 0.06-0.09. 0.10 is a realistic boundary a well-tuned run can stay below while still flagging genuinely discriminatory training.

dev_plans.content:
Statistically rigorous approach: Bootstrap-based confidence intervals on per-subgroup AUROC, AUPRC, sensitivity, specificity, and Brier score, with a hypothesis-test-driven disparity scalar.

What is measured: A multi-metric fairness audit. Per epoch on the validation set, for each of 6 ethnicity subgroups, compute AUROC, AUPRC, sensitivity at fixed specificity 0.9, specificity at fixed sensitivity 0.9, and Brier score. For each metric, run B=1000 stratified bootstrap resamples within each subgroup to obtain 95% CIs. The headline metric is a normalized maximum pairwise disparity in AUROC, scored against its bootstrap-derived null.

Pipeline insertion: Post-validation hook each epoch. To control cost, run full bootstrap every K epochs (e.g., K=5) and a cheap point estimate every epoch; the time-series metric is the per-epoch point estimate, with bootstrap CIs overlaid every K epochs.

Step-by-step:
1. Extend validation outputs to (y_true, y_pred_proba, ethnicity) arrays.
2. Define compute_metrics(y, p) -> dict of 5 metrics; vectorize where possible.
3. For bootstrap: numpy RNG with fixed seed per epoch (epoch as seed) for reproducibility. For each subgroup, draw B resamples with replacement preserving sample size; compute all 5 metrics per resample. Store percentile CIs (2.5/97.5).
4. Compute pairwise gaps for AUROC: for each pair (g_i, g_j), gap_ij = AUROC_i - AUROC_j. The disparity = max_ij |gap_ij|.
5. Significance test: under H0 that AUROCs are equal, the bootstrap distribution of (auroc_i - auroc_j) gives a p-value via the fraction of resamples with |gap| >= observed. Adjust with Bonferroni across 15 pairs.
6. Persist a JSON per epoch: {epoch, per_group: {metric: {point, ci_low, ci_high}}, disparity_auroc, max_gap_pvalue, smallest_group_n}.
7. Produce a forest-plot per epoch of subgroup AUROCs with CIs, and a line chart of disparity_auroc vs epoch with the threshold line. Add a second panel for AUPRC disparity for completeness.
8. Use stratified bootstrap (resample positives and negatives separately) to handle class imbalance in tiny groups.

Output artifacts: (a) JSON log per epoch; (b) matplotlib forest plots saved to runs/<run_id>/fairness/epoch_K.png; (c) time-series plot of AUROC disparity with bootstrap-CI band and horizontal threshold line; (d) Slack/email alert when disparity exceeds threshold for >=2 consecutive epochs after epoch 5.
dev_plans.metric: Maximum pairwise AUROC gap across all (6 choose 2)=15 ethnicity subgroup pairs on the validation set per epoch (max_{i,j} |AUROC_i - AUROC_j|).
dev_plans.threshold: 0.10 — Calibrated against MIMIC mortality/readmission benchmarks (Harutyunyan et al. 2019) where Native American and Asian subgroups have n<500 in the validation split. Bootstrap CI widths alone are typically 0.05-0.08 for these small groups, so the true disparity must exceed both noise and threshold to be flagged. Chen et al. NeurIPS 2018 and CheXclusion both treat ~10pp as the action-required boundary; 0.05 would fire constantly on bootstrap noise, while 0.15 is too lax. A well-tuned baseline with reweighted loss can plausibly reach <0.10.

dev_plans.content:
Tooling/library-based approach: Use Fairlearn + Aequitas to instrument the training pipeline with a standardized fairness MetricFrame, integrated into MLflow / Weights & Biases for dashboarding.

What is measured: A standardized fairness audit produced by Fairlearn's MetricFrame across the 6 ethnicity subgroups, exposing AUROC, AUPRC, sensitivity (TPR), specificity (TNR), and Brier score, plus Aequitas's disparity ratios. The headline scalar is Fairlearn's 'difference' aggregation on AUROC across subgroups.

Pipeline insertion: A custom Lightning/Keras Callback (or PyTorch ignite handler) invoked on validation_epoch_end. The callback wraps predictions in a Fairlearn MetricFrame keyed by sensitive_features=ethnicity.

Step-by-step:
1. Install dependencies: fairlearn>=0.10, aequitas>=1.0, mlflow or wandb. Confirm the validation Dataset yields ethnicity as a string label aligned with the MIMIC demographic schema.
2. Build a metric dict: {'auroc': lambda y,p: roc_auc_score(y,p), 'auprc': average_precision_score, 'sensitivity': lambda y,p: recall_score(y, p>=0.5), 'specificity': specificity_fn, 'brier': brier_score_loss}. AUROC/AUPRC/Brier consume probabilities; sensitivity/specificity consume thresholded predictions.
3. On validation_epoch_end: collect y_true, y_proba, ethnicity arrays. Construct mf = MetricFrame(metrics=metric_dict, y_true=y_true, y_pred=y_proba, sensitive_features=ethnicity).
4. Extract mf.by_group (DataFrame of per-group metrics) and mf.difference(method='between_groups') (Fairlearn's max-min gap). Use mf.ratio() for ratio-based disparities.
5. Pass the same arrays to Aequitas Group + Bias to compute disparity ratios (e.g., FPR disparity, FNR disparity) relative to the reference group 'White'. Aequitas auto-generates an HTML fairness report.
6. Log to MLflow / W&B: mlflow.log_metric('auroc_disparity', mf.difference()['auroc'], step=epoch). Log per-group metrics as separate keys (auroc_group_Hispanic, etc.) so W&B can plot them as overlapping curves.
7. Save the Aequitas HTML report as an MLflow artifact every 5 epochs.
8. In the W&B dashboard, configure a panel with the auroc_disparity time series and a horizontal reference line at the threshold using wandb.plot.line_series; also add a small-multiples panel of per-group AUROC.
9. Add an MLflow tag on run completion summarizing min/max/mean disparity across training.

Output artifacts: (a) MLflow / W&B logged scalar time series for disparity and per-group metrics; (b) Aequitas HTML fairness report as MLflow artifact; (c) Fairlearn MetricFrame DataFrame pickled per epoch for offline analysis; (d) dashboard panel showing disparity vs epoch with threshold reference line.
dev_plans.metric: Fairlearn MetricFrame.difference(method='between_groups') applied to AUROC across 6 ethnicity subgroups on the validation set per epoch (i.e., max-min AUROC gap, computed by the standardized Fairlearn API).
dev_plans.threshold: 0.10 — Matches Fairlearn's documented default rule-of-thumb for 'large' between-group differences in classification AUROC, and matches the disparity ratio of ~0.85-0.90 that Aequitas's '4/5 rule'-style audits flag. On MIMIC-style cohorts with imbalanced ethnicity counts, a competently trained baseline (cross-entropy + class weighting) typically achieves a between-groups AUROC difference in the 0.06-0.12 range, so 0.10 is a realistic, well-calibrated boundary that distinguishes a reasonably fair run from a clearly biased one without false-firing on bootstrap noise.
